taskDescription: Arrange the five layers of the scalp from superficial to deep using the SCALP mnemonic.
paragraphs: S — Skin: thick, hair-bearing, rich in sebaceous glands; site of pilar cysts.
C — Dense Connective tissue: contains vessels and nerves held open by fibrous septa, causing profuse bleeding when cut.
A — Aponeurosis (galea aponeurotica): fibromuscular sheet connecting frontalis anteriorly and occipitalis posteriorly; wounds here gape widely.
L — Loose areolar tissue: the dangerous layer; haematoma and pus spread freely here; communicates with the orbit via emissary veins.
P — Pericranium (periosteum): tightly bound to the outer skull table; cephalhaematoma (birth injury) is beneath this layer and does NOT cross sutures.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
